Title: FHIR Interoperability MVP
Description: Deliver a traceable relational-to-FHIR transformation proof of concept.
Acceptance Criteria: All child user stories completed and evidence attached.

Title: Map relational healthcare records to FHIR resources
Description: As a data analyst, I want source columns mapped to FHIR paths so that transformation logic is clear and reviewable.
Acceptance Criteria: A mapping document identifies source column, target resource, target path, rule, and notes; unsupported assumptions are documented.

Title: Generate FHIR R4 JSON resources
Description: As an integration analyst, I want valid JSON resources so that downstream API testing can be performed.
Acceptance Criteria: Notebook generates Patient, Encounter, Condition, Observation, MedicationStatement, Coverage, Claim, Practitioner, and Organization JSON resources.

Title: Create transaction bundles
Description: As a technical team member, I want transaction bundles so that multiple related resources can be submitted consistently.
Acceptance Criteria: Each selected encounter generates a Bundle with transaction entries and POST request metadata.

Title: Validate API payloads in Postman
Description: As a tester, I want reusable Postman requests and assertions so that payload and response structures can be validated.
Acceptance Criteria: Importable collection includes metadata, resource validation, transaction submission, and search requests with response tests.

Title: Demonstrate SQL and Databricks workflow
Description: As a data engineer, I want SQL queries and Spark transformations so that the approach can scale beyond local pandas analysis.
Acceptance Criteria: Notebook includes Spark DataFrame creation, temp view registration, SQL quality checks, and JSON export examples.

Title: Maintain traceability
Description: As a project stakeholder, I want requirements linked to artifacts so that implementation can be reviewed across the lifecycle.
Acceptance Criteria: Backlog rows reference notebook sections and generated mapping artifacts; acceptance criteria are testable.
